programmes.show_description: The Morning After: Knocked out in pursuit of a rogue agent, Steed awakes to the sight of a deserted London under the control of an embittered Brigadier with explosive plans.
Margaret's Engagement: Margaret's sudden engagement comes as a surprise to everyone, but Frank predictably takes it the hardest.
Out of Sight: Hawkeye is temporarily blinded while trying to fix the nurses' furnace, and Frank finds a sure-fire way to win bets on baseball games.
Lt Radar O'Riley: Following an offer of promotion made by Master Sergeant Woodruff at a poker game, Radar is promoted to the rank of lieutenant.
The Test: Tensions between the pair intensify when Henry reluctantly agrees to teach Matthew to drive.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Dust Child: Jonathan and Mark intervene after witnessing the prejudice facing a girl who has begun living with her father's family in the United States.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Hotel Of Dreams: Mark and Jonathan work at a hotel to help a millionaire's son dubbed Rudd the Dud find meaning in his life. They must also contend with a tyrannical boss and snooty guests.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Merry Christmas From Grandpa: On Christmas Eve, Jonathan shows the President, a farmer and an energy magnate what will happen in the future if the Earth continues to be ravaged by pollution.
To Touch The Moon: Jonathan counsels a young runaway and a widow with a terminally ill son. Meanwhile, Mark deals with a thief abandoned by his folks.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Gift: The Sunday school class decide to buy a brand-new Bible for Reverend Aldan's birthday.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: His Father's Son: Trying to interest his newly adopted son in more 'manly' pursuits, Isaiah Edwards buys a rifle for John Jr's birthday and insists that the two of them go hunting.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Talking Machine: A machine that records voices is just what treacherous Nellie Oleson needs to embarrass rival Laura when both girls vie for the same boy.
The Hawk: A fierce hawk threatens the community, and a handsome new minister comes to Walton's Mountain.
The Stray: Mysterious events leads to the discovery of a 12-year-old orphan boy, who attaches himself to John Walton.
Hello and Farewell: The village mourn the loss of Ambrose, who is laid to rest. Orla is looking for somewhere to live and Conor Devlin is a potential landlord.
A House Divided: Village pride is at stake when Ormston and Thursvale compete in the annual Pub Olympics. Meanwhile, Tom is unimpressed with his father's gift from New Zealand.
Dr Robert Bramwell's experiments with electrotherapy treatment land him in hot water. Meanwhile, Eleanor has her own problems when a patient is far from grateful for her help.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Age for Retirement: Rumpole's latest case sees him reacquainted with the Timson family. Can he prove to the court that his client has been framed for the theft of a painting?
The Nurses: The nurses go behind Margaret's back so that a fellow nurse confined to her tent can spend the night with her soldier husband who is paying her a surprise visit.
The Abduction: Colonel Flagg is called in to investigate when Margaret disappears during the release of North Korean prisoners in the area, unaware that she's gone to help deliver a baby.
Dear Sigmund: Psychiatrist Major Freedman writes a letter to Sigmund Freud about his frustrations and observations during the war while the rest of the camp endures a prankster's hijinks.
Action adventure with Clive Owen and Morgan Freeman. A medieval warrior emerges from exile to seek vengeance for his noble master, igniting a battle that will decide the fate of the kingdom. (2015)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

K Is For Kill Part 1: The Tiger Awakes: The team's latest investigation leads them to France, where a series of incidents have occurred. Have the sites been specifically chosen? Part 1 of 2.
The Nurses: The nurses go behind Margaret's back so that a fellow nurse confined to her tent can spend the night with her soldier husband who is paying her a surprise visit.
The Abduction: Colonel Flagg is called in to investigate when Margaret disappears during the release of North Korean prisoners in the area, unaware that she's gone to help deliver a baby.
Any Questions?: Henry makes the bold decision to run as a councillor in the upcoming local elections, but can he convince those closest to him to cast their vote for him?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Case of Identity: Can Rumpole successfully prove that his client is the victim of mistaken identity?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Another Song For Christmas: In this variation on 'A Christmas Carol', Jonathan and Mark have a Dickens of a time bringing Holiday joy into the heart of 'Honest Eddie', a crooked car dealer.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Plane Death: Mark and Jonathan visit an old friend of Mark's but discover he has been missing for five days and encounter conspiracy of silence as they search for clues.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

To See the World: Taking responsibility for filling Johnny Johnson's head with exaggerated tales of adventure, Mr Edwards accompanies Johnny when he takes off to see the world.
Founder's Day: Founder's Day festivities promise fun and friendly competition for all except ageing logger Jim Tyler, who fears losing the log chopping contest to a younger Charles Ingalls.
The Richest Man in Walnut Grove: Charles and his employer Mr Hansen are anticipating a large income from a big job. But when go wrong, the family feel the financial strain.
Four Eyes: The mysterious decline in Mary's school performance is explained when Charles discovers that she needs glasses - but Mary loses her confidence when her class begins to tease her.
Ebenezer Sprague: Laura Ingalls is stunned to learn that her friendly fishing friend is Walnut Grove's new banker and is upset when he accuses her of befriending him to secure a loan.
In the Big Inning: Walnut Grove's baseball team finally gets the chance to beat its main rival, but the town's lack of confidence in the team stirs up trouble for Slick McBurney.
Haunted House: Although many in Walnut Grove think he's a crazy man who lives in a haunted house, Laura befriends a mysterious hermit and soon unearths the truth behind his reclusive life.
The Spring Dance: Caroline is puzzled when her plan to make Isaiah Edwards jealous fails.
Remember Me: Part 1: Widow Julia Sanderson is unafraid when Doc Baker diagnoses her with a terminal illness, but she knows she must help her three children cope with the news. Part 1 of 2.
Remember Me: Part 2: When it appears that no family can adopt all three of the Sanderson children together, a weary Charles fears he must break his promise to their mother. Part 2 of 2.
The Camp Out: Despite a rocky start, the Olesons accompany the Ingalls on a family camping trip, and surprisingly, the feuding families enjoy each other's company.
At the End of the Rainbow: Laura swears her friend Jonah to secrecy after the two uncover what appears to be gold ore lying on the bed of their favourite fishing hole.
The Gift: The Sunday school class decide to buy a brand-new Bible for Reverend Aldan's birthday.
A Dark Blue Perfume: Cold case whodunnit with Janet Suzman. Cecily retires to the seaside town of her youth, but she becomes obsessed with unravelling a decades-old murder.
The Orchard Walls: Wartime drama starring Honeysuckle Weeks, Fiona Dolman and Sylvia Syms. Forbidden love leads to tragedy when a teenager is sent to her aunt's home during the Blitz.
Rumpole and the Case of Identity: Can Rumpole successfully prove that his client is the victim of mistaken identity?
